items.title: [Deploy-S3+S4] Bare-metal deploy targets (OsServicePort + SystemdAdapter + deno compile) — PLAN

milestone.title: 0.0.1-beta.1
milestone.description:
First beta of the 0.0.1 line — a MINIMAL cuttable release of the current green state. The 0.0.1 line ships on an incremental-beta cadence (beta.1 -> beta.2 -> ... -> stable), mirroring the alpha line (alpha.1..alpha.19: 19 incremental prereleases). beta.1 is NOT a positioning gate; it publishes the framework as it stands today so the beta channel opens and later bets land incrementally.

Cut bar (all satisfied on main as of 2026-07-03):
1. Soundness floor — plugin-service contract seam type-sound end-to-end (172a-2-SOUND, #332 CLOSED).
2. Instrument honesty — bench `conformance` gate green (cast-free NetScript reference passes the frozen HTTP suite 10/10 with a real process restart proving @netscript/kv persistence; CI-guarded every run). Satisfied floor, not blocked work.
3. Scaffold trust — `scaffold.runtime` e2e green (48/0).
4. CI trio green — quality / check-test / deps-report succeed on main.
5. Release machinery works — `deno task release:cut` (workspace+lock bump, preflight, publish:dry-run, ci --prod) and the `publish.yml` OIDC publish path, both proven across alpha.1..alpha.19.

MOVED OUT of beta.1 (were in the prior LOCKED heavy bar):
- self-bench t1+t2 test_pass_rate >=0.90 median -> beta.2 (regression-detector quality bar; blocked on bench decisions D5/OQ2).
- competitor-PAIRED run, multi-task breadth, full composite rubric -> 0.0.1-stable (the falsifiable positioning verdict).

Note: allow-slow-types is a sanctioned doctrine exception for oRPC-bound packages (S2; S4/#305) and does not count against the soundness/publish bar. Positioning verdict is a stable-line goal reached incrementally; see packages/bench/POSITIONING.md (owned by the road-to-stable lane).

items.body:
## Bare-metal deploy targets (Deploy-S3 + S4) — IMPLEMENTED

Delivers epic #327's **bare-metal deployment** lane as a single coherent Archetype-7 slice:
generalize `WindowsServicePort` → `OsServicePort` + add a `SystemdAdapter` (**#339**, S3), and adopt
a `deno compile` single-binary bare-metal artifact (**#340**, S4).

> This PR was originally opened planning-only; it now carries the **full implementation** (S0 port
> expansion through the bare-metal targets), rebased onto `0.0.1-beta.1` `main`. The
> `scaffold.runtime` merge-readiness gate is **GREEN** (`passed=48 failed=0`).

### What landed
- **`OsServicePort`** OS service-lifecycle seam (`public/ports/os-service-port.ts`) with two
  adapters: `ServyOsServiceAdapter` (Windows, `public/adapters/servy-os-service.ts`) and
  `SystemdOsServiceAdapter` (Linux, `public/adapters/systemd-os-service.ts`). Kernel systemd
  arg-builders/renderers live in `kernel/adapters/linux/systemd/**` (pure, no port dependency).
- **Bare-metal `DeployTargetPort` targets** (`kernel/domain/deploy/service-deploy-target.ts` +
  linux/windows subclasses) that **compose** the OsServicePort + a `deno compile` build for 7-op
  conformance — evolving the shipped `windows-service` stub, not a parallel reimplementation.
- **`deno compile`** single-binary artifact (S4): `kernel/adapters/deploy/compile/**`
  (`compile-platform.ts`, `runtime-detect.ts`).
- Config: `deploy.targets.linux` added by spreading the base shape (`packages/config`).

### Contract
Archetype 7's uniform **7-op** adapter contract (`plan`/`emit` · `up` · `down` · `status` · `logs` ·
`rollback` · `secrets`, subset allowed). This slice implements the bare-metal
**`plan`/`up`/`down`/`status`/`logs`** subset. **`rollback` + `secrets` are declared-unsupported
(methods omitted, not silent no-ops) and deferred to the core-convention hardening slice #341** —
tests assert `rollback === undefined` / `secrets === undefined`. Legacy CLI verbs
(`build`/`install`/`uninstall`) are retained as thin-router aliases (zero CLI-UX churn).

### Key decisions (LD-1..LD-8 — delivered)
- **LD-1** two-layer OsServicePort seam + composing bare-metal targets + compile build.
- **LD-2** clean rename `WindowsServicePort` → `OsServicePort` (old files deleted, zero dangling refs).
- **LD-3** canonical 7-op names; legacy verbs as thin-router aliases.
- **LD-6** stays inside `packages/cli` (+ `packages/config` schema) — **no `deploy-core` extraction**
  this wave; **no `deno.lock` churn**.

### Verification
- `scaffold.runtime` merge-readiness E2E: **GREEN** (`passed=48 failed=0`, `SCAFFOLD_RUNTIME_EXIT=0`)
  on the rebased head.
- Separate-session adversarial review: **PASS** — layering clean (no `kernel → public/**` import),
  type-soundness clean (post-review the one redundant safe cast was removed), rollback/secrets
  deferral clean.
- Note: user-facing `deploy start/stop/status` verbs remain Windows-gated; Linux execution wiring
  onto the OsServicePort seam is deferred to #341 (the targets/adapters exist and are registered).

## SKILL
- `netscript-doctrine` — Archetype 7 (Deployment Target Adapter) placement, gates, debt.
- `netscript-cli` — CLI/deploy command surface, thin-router conventions.
- `netscript-harness` — harnessed slice workflow, drift/worklog artifacts.
- `netscript-pr` — closing keywords, taxonomy labels, milestone.

Closes #339
Closes #340
Refs #327
Refs #341

Part of #327

**Handle:** #E6 · **Milestone:** `0.0.1-beta.8` · **Depends on:** #E2 (shell) + #E4 (single-process artifact to package). **Blocks:** #E7, #E8.

## Scope — 1-click packaging + release/update server

`deno desktop` cross-compile (`--target`/`--all-targets`, no Rust toolchain, SHA-256-verified prebuilt targets; `--compress xz|zstd`; `--no-check`; explicit `-o`); release server serving `latest.json` + bsdiff deltas + Ed25519-signed manifests; **Windows manual-apply fallback** (relaunch-installer indirection — Tauri/Electron pattern) for the stages-not-applies gap; local structural type layer over `Deno.BrowserWindow`/`Tray`/`autoUpdate`/`desktopVersion`.

## Acceptance

- Reproducible signed binary per target; `Deno.autoUpdate()` stages+applies on macOS/Linux and stages+manual-applies on Windows; manifests signature-verified before staging; desktop-globals layer is lint-clean and a no-op in the web build.

Design source: `design/E-desktop/epic-and-issues.md` (#E6).

items.body:
## Summary

No deploy target has any end-to-end coverage. `scaffold.runtime` exercises workers/sagas/triggers/streams plugins but no deploy target, and there is no separate deploy e2e suite anywhere in `packages/cli/e2e/`. Combined with the false-closed deploy acceptances in beta.2 (#343 unregistered, #342 mock-only), the deploy lane's runtime correctness is unverified.

Found by the beta.3→stable roadmap re-forecast evidence sweep (#391), verified against the `v0.0.1-beta.2` tag.

## Evidence (baseline `1b42ba88`)

- `packages/cli/e2e/src/domain/extension-axes.ts` PLUGIN enum = WORKER/SAGA/TRIGGER/STREAM/AUTH — no deploy axis.
- `runtime-gates.ts` has behavior gates for workers/sagas/triggers only — no deploy gate.
- No deploy target appears in the scaffold.runtime suite; no `deploy.*` e2e suite exists.

## Ask

Add at least one deploy target to an e2e gate (Deno Deploy or bare-metal systemd — whichever is chosen as the stable "verified production deployment path"), so the deploy lane has real acceptance coverage. This is a prerequisite for #301's stable "≥1 verified production deployment path + CI deployability gate."

## Scope

- Archetype / area: packages/cli e2e
- Part of #327
- Refs #345

_Filed by the roadmap re-forecast sweep; not fixed in that planning PR._

items.title: epic: Road to 0.0.1-stable

milestone.title: 0.0.1-stable
milestone.description:
0.0.1 stable. Acceptance criteria LOCKED (packages/bench/POSITIONING.md section 4, per #353 + user decisions on S1/S2). Stable extends beta with the falsifiable positioning verdict:
1. All beta criteria hold.
2. Competitor-PAIRED run — at least one measured paired run (NetScript vs >=1 D2 alternative) where NetScript is not worse on test_pass_rate AND strictly better on >=1 efficiency axis (turns_to_green / cost / wall_seconds). Moved here from beta per S1 decision (blocked on D2/D5/OQ2).
3. Breadth — multi-task coverage (>=2 distinct build tasks) and N>=3 runs per cell for a stable median.
4. Full rubric — composite scoring complete (0.20 reserve retired; bench Slice 5 rebind onto the sound baseContract landed).
Note: allow-slow-types remains a sanctioned doctrine exception for oRPC-bound packages (S2; S4/#305).

items.body:
Program epic tracking everything between `0.0.1-alpha.19` and a credible `0.0.1-beta.x`, then `0.0.1-stable`. Supervised by the road-to-stable program session; the coordinator holds the merge gate.

## Locked positioning

NetScript competes on **AI-agent build-efficiency, not runtime throughput**. Defensible falsifiable claim: *one-primitive-per-concern + compile-time contract enforcement + explicit plugin composition let an AI agent ship production-grade backend code in fewer turns with less hand-rolled code than bare routers, and at production-readiness parity-or-better vs the batteries-included tier (Encore.ts).* This sidesteps the RPS fight and plays where the evidence is winnable. Falsifiable via `turns_to_green` + `production_rubric_pass_rate` (t1/t2), low-variance `test_pass_rate` (t1), and a reactive Fresh-UI task (t3b) as a **separate extended axis**. We do **not** claim shipped AI-backend capability (in-flight #238), throughput leadership, or API stability yet. Credibility asset: fork Encore's exact weights, publish raw traces, disclose the alpha-corpus + Fresh-2-beta confounds openly. Source: validated `netscript-bench-research.v2.md`.

## Locked acceptance criteria (bench numbers = post-Run-1 "match-or-beat peer median" placeholders)

**`0.0.1-beta.x` — correctness floor + repo maturity**
- Self-bench exists; Run-1 t1+t2 `test_pass_rate` >= 0.90 median; t1 `turns_to_green` <= NestJS median; t1 `production_rubric` >= 0.50; zero harness-level NetScript failures.
- `e2e-cli-prod` + `scaffold.runtime` green (hard gates); plugin-service type-soundness fixed (172a-2-SOUND); public surface doc-linted; zero machine-specific assumptions in shipped tooling; doctrine v2 ratified with fitness functions in `arch:check`; stale-elimination wave-1 complete; one-shot deterministic `release:cut` proven by cutting the beta itself.

**`0.0.1-stable` — leadership + production-readiness**
- t2 `turns_to_green` strictly below all bare routers and <= Encore.ts; `production_rubric` >= 0.80 and `test_pass_rate` >= 0.95 on t1-t3; composite top-2 among core-5; reproduced across Sonnet 5 + Opus 4.8; t3b >= 0.90.
- Full cross-framework batch published; continuous self-bench per release; API/semver policy + public-surface-diff CI gate; deprecation policy enforced; >=1 verified production deployment path + CI deployability gate; zero unowned arch-debt.

## Children (sub-epics)

- [ ] #302 — S1 Positioning + `netscript-bench`
- [ ] #303 — S2 Enterprise maturation + consolidation
- [x] #304 — S3 De-`rickylabs` / machine-agnostic tooling (closed 2026-07-03)
- [ ] #305 — S4 Doctrine revamp
- [ ] #306 — S5 Harness + skills revamp
- [ ] #307 — S6 Stale-code + stale-file elimination
- [ ] #327 — S7 Deployment (**dedicated Deployment Fable epic**, now filed & unblocked; child of this umbrella, not owned or designed here). SERVY verdict = MODERNIZE; tier-1 beta targets = Deno Deploy + `deno compile` bare-metal + Docker/Compose; Aspire container/cloud lane critical-path-gated on `ada2a5` AddDenoApp seam #320 (Phases 0–2 unblocked). Satisfies the **stable** milestone's "≥1 verified production deployment path + CI deployability gate." Supersedes the #308 tracking pointer.
- [ ] #309 — S8 Release engineering + API-stability gates
- [ ] #313 — DB-layer foundation: Prisma-Next migration (deferred; spec + gap-tracker only, impl deferred; Postgres-first additive pilot, multi-db guarantee holds, MSSQL stays classic path). Tracked child with a **locked design — NOT a beta blocker**. Gap sub-issues: #314 MSSQL · #315 Standard-Schema validator consumer (Zod-deletion unlock) · #316 MySQL · #317 Turso/libsql · #318 beta/GA channel.
- [ ] #391 — beta.3→stable re-forecast epic (roadmap-expansion seed run; milestone train authority)
- [ ] #399 — telemetry-revamp epic (T1–T9; beta.5 slice = #402/#403, remainder beta.6)
- [ ] #400 — Dev Dashboard epic (beta.6; DDX-8 co-lands with telemetry T4–T7)
- [ ] #401 — docs-cut epic (beta.7)

## Tracked in-flight lanes (owned elsewhere — referenced, not stomped)

- Docs-site quality overhaul: #232 / #264 / #278 (owns all docs authoring; doc gaps route there). Handed-off follow-ups tracked here: **Group A** — Storefront tutorial Run-2 (release-cut-gated, Refs #153); **Group B** — `how-to/customize-fresh-ui.md` prompt-input/dropzone copy refresh (enhancement).
- AI stack: #238 (converging; consolidate as it lands, do not re-architect mid-flight)
- Aspire Deno-runtime proof: #295 (evidence-only)
- DB layer: owned by child epic **#313** (Prisma-Next migration) — carved out of every stale/maturation sweep; any DB-layer finding routes to #313, never edited inline here.
- GitHub-native process layer (milestones/RFC/templates/labels/Projects/Discussions/community-health — owned by a dedicated process lane)

## Operating rules

Focused reviewable PRs only, never a mega-PR. **Every branch cuts from `origin/main`** (the working checkout ran 380 commits behind during audit). Doctrine-first for `packages/`/`plugins/`; contract-first; wrap-don't-reinvent. Implementer and evaluator are separate sessions; CI `quality` trio (`fmt:check`+`lint`+`check`, workspace `check` needs `--unstable-kv`) reproduced before every PR; scaffold-affecting changes run `deno task e2e:cli run scaffold.runtime --cleanup --format pretty`. `e2e-cli-prod` stays a hard release gate. Publishing stays in CI via OIDC. No backward-compat pre-beta: stale code is deleted, not shimmed.

## Stale-elimination methodology note (S6/#307)

Deleting a `.css`/asset file requires a **class-name-coupling** check (does any shipped component apply the BEM/selectors it defines via `cn(...)`/`class=`?), not only an `@import`/`<link>`/registry-import grep. Wave-1 nearly dropped `fresh-ui/src/presentation/data-grid.css`, which styles the shipped exported `DataGrid` (#225) purely by `ns-data-grid*` class-name coupling — invisible to import-grep and to `deno check` (CSS is not type-checked). Restored before merge (PR #324).

## User decisions pending (bench §12)

D1 VM provider · D2 framework list · D3 task count · D4 score weights · D5 model+cadence · D6 repo location · D7 license. Plus product escalations: beta/stable version-surface strategy; `--legacy-aspire` removal approval; `packages/runtime-config` roadmap-or-retire. From #313 (DB layer, coordinator-held): default-flip policy · Postgres-only-vs-all-engines matrix policy · validator-branch confirmation. NetScript self-bench proceeds without any of these.

- Deployment near-term: target-adapter archetype doctrine (#338 S2), Deno Deploy tier-1 adapter — marquee p0 (#342 S6), docs/code divergence fix (#344 S8).

items.body:
## Summary

Cut NetScript **0.0.1-beta.2**.

This bumps the root version, every workspace member (`packages/*`, `plugins/*`, nested workspaces), and the `deno.lock` `@netscript/*` ranges from `0.0.1-beta.1` to `0.0.1-beta.2`. It also reconciles `@netscript/ai` off its stale `alpha.0` meta-version onto the release line.

`@netscript/ai` and `@netscript/plugin-ai-core` were re-enabled for JSR publish just ahead of this cut (revert of #362 via the preceding PR), so beta.2 is the first release to ship the **AI foundation** to JSR. Note: the userland `@netscript/plugin-ai` scaffolder stays `publish:false` — it is not in the published surface; its flagship parity (e2e/hardening/`/v1/ai` impl) lands in beta.3 (#388).

## What beta.2 ships (highlights since beta.1)

- **AI foundation published to JSR** — model providers (Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, OpenRouter, Ollama), agent loop + `ChatClientPort`, MCP transport, embeddings/vision, tool system, and the plugin manifest/connector/codegen contract (in `@netscript/plugin-ai-core`). The `@netscript/plugin-ai` plugin itself is not yet published.
- **Shared cache provisioner** — Deno KV Connect + Docker-less Garnet (executable arm), engine×mode matrix (#371, #372).
- **Deployment targets** — Deno Deploy tier-1 adapter, bare-metal (systemd + `deno compile`), Aspire Docker/Compose, secrets/rollback seam.
- **Docs-site beta.2 overhaul** — AI stack, durable chat, Deno Deploy.

## Validation

`deno task release:cut -- 0.0.1-beta.2` ran the full ordered gate set on the bumped tree:

- Version bump + residue check — no `0.0.1-beta.1` residue remains in JSON / `deno.lock`.
- `release:preflight` — PASS (text-imports, file-url-import-meta, self-imports).
- `publish:dry-run` — PASS across all publishable members (incl. `@netscript/ai`, `@netscript/plugin-ai-core`).
- `deno ci --prod` — PASS (prod graph installs from the locked graph).

## Post-Merge (owner action)

Publishing stays in GitHub Actions via OIDC — never local. After merge:

```
deno task release:publish -- v0.0.1-beta.2 --notes-file <intro.md>
```

This creates the `v0.0.1-beta.2` GitHub Release (Latest, non-prerelease), which triggers `publish.yml` (OIDC → JSR for every `@netscript/*` member) and then `e2e-cli-prod.yml` (installs the published CLI, runs the prod scaffold smoke). Release is done only when **both** `publish.yml` and `e2e-cli-prod` are green.

items.title: RFC: single deployment — enterprise installation layer, update lifecycle, PM foundation, single-runtime composition

labels.name: rfc
labels.description: Request for Comments - substantial/breaking design change

milestone.title: Backlog / Triage
milestone.description: Catch-all for accepted-but-unscheduled work and untriaged issues. Assign to a release milestone once scoped.

items.body:
Owner-mandated RFC (2026-07-17), grounded in the eis-chat POC (rickylabs/eis-chat#150, merge `aeaf2df` — "Prototype full-stack Windows singleton desktop deployment": no-Docker Windows `--singleton` around Deno Desktop, native window directly supervising the adjacent service graph, Garnet + six compiled Deno processes, Aspire sidecar telemetry, shipped as a single `.msi`).

**The POC proves feasibility. This RFC extracts what SHOULD BE BUILT as foundational layers — we learn from the POC's seams, PRs, issues, and fixes; we do not copy its scripts into NetScript.**

## Findings that motivate this RFC

1. **Process manager is the missing foundation (beta.12 / epic #510).** The POC's supervision is launch-only: if a sidecar service dies, the frontend is unaware and the only recovery is re-opening the app. Crash survival, restart policy, health propagation to the UI, and lifecycle guarantees must exist BEFORE any desktop deployment ships on NetScript. Sequencing: PM epic (beta.12) is a hard prerequisite of the desktop deployment work.
2. **Installation layer is a gap in BOTH epics.** The POC ships as a simple .NET AOT host. An enterprise-grade installation layer must handle much more (install/uninstall/repair, per-machine vs per-user, service registration, permissions/elevation, signing, first-run provisioning) and must be integrated WITHIN the Aspire stack, not bolted on outside it. Today this sits in neither the PM epic (#510) nor the desktop packaging issues (e.g. PM-32 #543) — scope it and place it.
3. **Update-management lifecycle is unanswered for the combined output.** Deno Desktop ships an update story for a single desktop output; our artifact is a combined singleton (window + supervised service graph + sidecars). How updates apply atomically/safely across that graph (staged rollout, rollback, partial-update hazards, service downtime windows) needs a designed answer.
4. **Composition with the single-runtime feature.** Prior single-runtime issues describe another packaging approach we intend to KEEP alongside the singleton desktop output. The RFC must define how the two compose (shared contracts? shared PM? divergent installers?) rather than letting them fork.

## Deliverables

- Gap analysis of epic #510 (PM) and the desktop packaging scope against the POC learnings, with concrete sub-issue adjustments (add/re-scope/re-milestone proposals for beta.12/beta.13 — proposals, owner ratifies).
- RFC design for the enterprise installation layer inside the Aspire stack.
- RFC design for update lifecycle of the combined singleton output.
- Composition contract between single-runtime and singleton-desktop approaches.

Run: `.llm/runs/rfc-single-deployment--orchestrator/` (Fable 5 · high generator, adversarial Sol · max). No implementation from this RFC; board changes are drafts until owner ratification.

Refs #510, #543, #327, #400.

items.title: fix(plugin-quality): clear IMPL-EVAL caveats AP-23 + A3 (#193)
items.body:
## Summary

Clears the two IMPL-EVAL caveats carried out of the merged plugin re-architecture / triggers-oRPC work (#193), so the next release cut is caveat-clean.

### AP-23 — exported `any` in `plugin-workers-core`

`PublicDefinitionSchemaShape` was `Readonly<Record<string, any>>` behind a `deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any`. It is now `Readonly<Record<string, z.ZodTypeAny>>` — `z.ZodTypeAny` is the idiomatic "any Zod schema" element type (already used elsewhere in the package), so the shape stays structurally open for consumers with **no `any`, no cast, and the lint-ignore directive removed**.

### A3 — README TS-fence hygiene

Eight plugin/core READMEs carried only one runnable TS example. Each now has a **second grounded TS fence**. Every added example was extracted and `deno check --unstable-kv`'d against the real import map, so the docs stay executable:

- `packages/plugin-workers-core`, `packages/plugin-sagas-core`, `packages/plugin-streams-core`, `packages/plugin-triggers-core`
- `plugins/workers`, `plugins/sagas`, `plugins/triggers`, `plugins/streams`

## Gates

- `run-deno-check` / `run-deno-lint` / `run-deno-fmt` (ts,tsx) over `packages` + `plugins` — green
- 8-README `deno fmt` — green
- doctrine sweep — `FAIL=0`, AP-23 and A3 cleared with no new findings

## Scope

9 files, `+205 / -2`. One source line changed (`public-schema.ts`); the rest are README additions. No lock churn, no generated-file churn.

items.title: ci: docs-only + label-gated skip lanes for expensive CI

milestone.title: 0.0.1-beta.5
milestone.description: Deployment hardening + repo/process maturity (staging boundary before stable)

items.body:
## Summary

Makes the expensive `e2e-cli` CI tier **skippable for docs-only PRs** and via
**explicit skip labels**, so documentation and low-risk chore PRs stop paying for
the aspire/docker/postgres scaffold-runtime E2E and the deno-only scaffold gate.
Owner addendum, `beta5-impl--supervisor` run.

Only `.github/workflows/e2e-cli.yml` gains skip behavior. The cheap required trio
and the release gates are deliberately untouched.

Refs #301.

## What changed

1. **`.github/scripts/ci-classify-changes.ts`** — a pure, unit-tested classifier
   (`decide()`) mapping a PR's changed-file set + labels to run/skip decisions.
   Denylist (`packages/`, `plugins/`, `apps/`, any `deno.json*`/`deno.lock`,
   `.github/workflows/`) **always wins** over the docs allowlist; unknown paths
   conservatively force the gate to run. 16 tests cover the taxonomy + label
   precedence.
2. **`.github/workflows/e2e-cli.yml`** — a new `classify` job diffs the PR
   merge-base and runs the classifier; the two expensive jobs consume its outputs.
3. **`.github/labels.yml`** — adds `ci:skip-scaffold`, refines `ci:full` /
   `ci:skip-e2e` descriptions. (No live label sync — separate owned process.)

## Skip matrix (workflow → job → skip condition → required-check impact)

| Workflow     | Job                | Skips when                                             | Required check? | On skip |
| ------------ | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ | --------------- | ------- |
| `e2e-cli`    | `classify`         | never (cheap; drives the others)                       | No              | n/a |
| `e2e-cli`    | `scaffold-static`  | docs-only diff **or** `ci:skip-scaffold` (not `ci:full`)| No             | job runs, short-circuits to SUCCESS |
| `e2e-cli`    | `scaffold-runtime` | docs-only diff **or** `ci:skip-e2e` (not `ci:full`)    | No              | job runs, short-circuits to SUCCESS |
| `ci`         | `check-test`, `quality`, `deps-report` | **never** (untouched)                | **Yes**         | always runs |
| `publish`    | all                | **never** (release gate, untouched)                    | —               | always runs |
| `e2e-cli-prod`, `e2e-cli-prod-local` | all | **never** (hard release gates, untouched)     | —               | always runs |
| `pages`      | all                | **never** (push-to-main docs deploy, untouched)        | —               | always runs |

**Label precedence:** `ci:full` > `ci:skip-scaffold`/`ci:skip-e2e` > docs-only
auto-detection > run.

## Docs-only safe set

A file is docs-only iff it matches the allowlist **and not** the denylist:

- **Allowlist:** `**/*.md`, `**/*.mdx`, and anything under `docs/`, `.llm/`,
  `.agents/`, `.claude/`.
- **Denylist (always impacting, wins):** `packages/**`, `plugins/**`, `apps/**`,
  any `deno.json` / `deno.jsonc` / `deno.lock` at any depth, `.github/workflows/**`.

The e2e harness itself lives at `packages/cli/e2e/` — already on the denylist — so
a change to what the gate exercises can never be misclassified as docs-only. A
`README.md` under `packages/` is likewise treated as impacting (denylist wins),
per the hard rule. Empty diffs and `workflow_dispatch` run the full gate.

## No-strand pattern (chosen: run-and-short-circuit-to-SUCCESS)

`main`'s ruleset (`required_status_checks`) currently requires **`quality`,
`check-test`, `deps-report`** only — all cheap `ci.yml` jobs. `scaffold-static`
and `scaffold-runtime` are **not** required today, so a policy skip cannot strand
the merge gate.

Rather than `paths-ignore:` (which suppresses the check context entirely and
breaks required checks) or a job-level `if:` skip (reports "skipped"), each
expensive job **always starts and reports SUCCESS**, guarding every work step on
`env.RUN == 'true'` and emitting a "Skipped by policy" `::notice::` step
otherwise. This keeps the design correct **even if these jobs are later promoted
to required checks** — the context always reports green. A single reusable
`classify` job feeds both, so there is no scattered path filtering.

The workflow's applicability `if` (PR to `main`, `e2e-cli-gate` label, or manual
dispatch) is unchanged and lives on `classify`; the expensive jobs `need` it, so
non-applicable PRs still skip exactly as before.

## Safety rails

- `publish.yml`, `e2e-cli-prod.yml`, `e2e-cli-prod-local.yml` (hard release
  gates) and `ci.yml` (required trio) get **no** skip path.
- Post-merge `push` to `main` behavior is unchanged (these workflows only alter
  `pull_request`/`workflow_dispatch` paths).

## Validation

- `deno test .github/scripts/ci-classify-changes.test.ts` — **16 passed**.
- `deno fmt --check` + `deno lint` on both scripts — clean.
- Workflow + `labels.yml` parse-checked via `@std/yaml`.
- End-to-end CLI check (env → `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`) for docs-only, code, `ci:skip-e2e`,
  markdown-under-packages, and dispatch scenarios — all correct.
- `actionlint` not available in the WSL toolchain (noted); relied on YAML parse +
  manual expression review of `env.RUN` step guards, job-level `env` from `needs`,
  the heredoc `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`, and `toJSON(labels.*.name)`.

## Notes for reviewer

- Pre-existing drift (out of scope, not touched): the opt-in **label name** in the
  workflow applicability `if` is `e2e-cli-gate`, but `labels.yml` defines
  `gate:e2e`. Left as-is to keep this diff minimal.
- No `area:ci` label exists; used `area:tooling` (nearest area).


## Adversarial-review fixes (round 1, commits a2f7f65 + f022358)

All three CAVEATS from the [PHASE: ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW] comment are fixed:

1. **Rename hole closed.** The diff step now uses `git diff --name-status -M`
   and the classifier's `parseNameStatus()` feeds **both** sides of `R*`/`C*`
   (and keeps `D` deleted paths). Regression test covers the exact
   `packages/cli/a.ts -> docs/a.md` case; reproduced in a temp git repo that
   the old `--name-only` showed only `docs/a.md` while the new form yields
   `run_static=true run_runtime=true docs_only=false`.

2. **Fail-closed rule.** A skip now requires `classify` to **succeed** with an
   explicit `run_*=false`. Downstream jobs run on
   `!cancelled() && needs.classify.result != 'skipped'` and compute
   `RUN: needs.classify.result != 'success' || outputs.run_* == 'true'` — so a
   failed classify (checkout/setup/self-check/output failure) forces the full
   expensive gates instead of silently skipping via needs-semantics. A
   `skipped` classify remains the pre-existing applicability gate (non-main
   base without `e2e-cli-gate`), unchanged.

3. **Injection-safe notices.** The skipped-by-policy steps no longer
   interpolate classifier output into shell source: the reason is passed via
   `env:` and printed with `printf '%s\n' "$REASON"`. Defense-in-depth:
   `sanitizeReason()` strips control characters/newlines (blocking crafted
   filenames from injecting extra `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` assignments) and caps
   length; verified a `$(touch pwned)`-style filename produces exactly 4
   output lines and executes nothing.

Re-validation: 25/25 classifier tests; fmt + lint clean; workflow YAML
parse-checked; 8 CLI scenario sims (docs-only, code, skip labels,
md-under-packages, dispatch, source→docs rename, docs→docs rename, source
delete) all correct.

items.body:
Closes #403

## What
T2 of the telemetry revamp: package ports/adapters restructure, closing the
`packages/telemetry` doctrine Refactor arch-debt entry (F-3/F-5/F-6).

- **Structure (F-3):** forbidden `core/` removed — types → `src/domain/`, span/tracer
  lifecycle → `src/application/`, registry lifecycle → `src/application/registry/`;
  provider ports in `src/ports/`, OTEL adapters in `src/adapters/otel/`; dead
  `instrumentation/sse.ts` (447 ln, zero consumers) deleted; orphan `src/public/mod.ts`
  confirmed absent. `application` never imports `adapters`.
- **Surface (F-5):** new `./otel` (TracerProviderPort + real Deno-native provider +
  documented SDK scaffold), `./query` (read-model contract + scaffold reader), and
  `./testing` (InMemorySpanRecorder) subpaths; `./registry` given a real facade;
  root `mod.ts` barrel completed with the primary tracing surface.
- **Publishability (F-6):** workspace-mutator JSR rewrite-map now covers the root and
  every telemetry subpath; env config validated with a hand-rolled Standard Schema
  (`telemetryConfigSchema`), failing fast on a malformed OTLP endpoint.

## Slices
1. `06825174` — kill forbidden `core/`, relocate to domain/application, delete dead sse.ts
2. `44b6a3a2` — ports/adapters subpath surface (otel/query/testing/registry + rewrite-map)
3. `ea6edd66` — Standard Schema env-config validation + fail-fast TelemetryConfigError
4. `f63ffcb6` — README subpaths, consumer-compat evidence, arch-debt closure

## Evidence
- `deno doc --lint` clean on the full 11-entrypoint export map; `deno publish --dry-run` Success.
- Repo-wide `deno check` over `packages/` + `plugins/`: 2242 files, 0 failures (all consumers compile).
- Telemetry tests 21/21 (T1 TC-1..14 convention contract preserved) + cli workspace-mutator 9/9.
- Scoped fmt/lint clean; no new `as`/`any` casts; `deno.lock` delta = 1 legitimate line
  (`@standard-schema/spec` for telemetry).
- Arch-debt entry closed in `.llm/harness/debt/arch-debt.md` with per-gate resolution.

## Deferred (scoped out of T2 by #403)
- SDK provider registration (T3) and the real Aspire query reader (T7) ship as documented
  throwing scaffolds behind stable contracts.

items.title: feat(cli): streams end-to-end CLI — implement the five stubbed runtime verbs and add schema/producer/consumer scaffolders

milestone.title: 0.0.1-beta.9
milestone.description: Road to stable: hardening, release-engineering gates, docs accuracy

items.body:
Part of the CLI-features-coverage epic #701.

## Context

Streams is the worst coverage asymmetry in the CLI. Both halves of the surface are missing:

**Runtime verbs are declared but dead.** All five commands in `plugins/streams/src/cli/streams-cli.ts:59-70` are stubs: `list-topics` returns code 0 with `topics: []` and "Stream topic walker is not implemented yet."; `subscribe`, `publish`, `stats`, and `clear` all return code 1 "not implemented until the streams runtime CLI is wired." Yet `docs/site/reference/streams/index.md:82-90` documents the `/cli` subpath as a shipped command group, and the only test asserts `commands().length === 5` (`plugins/streams/tests/cli/streams-cli_test.ts:29`) — none execute.

**There is no scaffolder at all** — unlike workers and triggers, which both ship `add-*` verbs:
- Schemas are hand-authored: `docs/site/how-to/publish-a-durable-stream.md:26-44` hand-writes `streams/shipment-stream-schema.ts` via `defineStreamSchema`; `docs/site/capabilities/streams.md:108` hand-authors `streams/executions-schema.ts`. No generate command or template references streams.
- Producers are hand-authored per plugin (`plugins/{auth,workers,sagas,triggers}/streams/producer.ts`); `capabilities/streams.md:113` teaches hand-writing `createDurableStream`, and the manifest sugar `defineStreamProducer` is itself a throwing stub (`plugins/streams/src/public/stream-api.ts:45-52`).
- Consumers are hand-authored end to end: `docs/site/tutorials/live-dashboard/05-live-stream.md:61-178` walks through hand-writing the StreamDB factory, live island, and stream loaders.

**Diagnostics exist but are unreachable:** core ships `inspectStreamTopic()` returning a JSON-stable report (`capabilities/streams.md:262`), but `streams stats` is a stub and no plugin doctor calls it; producer connect failures are only visible via console.warn scraping (`capabilities/streams.md:304-309`).

## Dashboard consumer

The merged dashboard design (PR #685, `.llm/runs/dashboard-design--orchestrator/`) specifies Streams as a full management console: `/streams` -> `/streams/:streamId` (`?tab=deliveries|subscribers|wiring`) -> `/subscribers/:subscriberId`, with per-message fan-out verdicts ("`msg_88f`: 2/3 delivered - 1 failed") and operable writes "Redeliver to failed subscriber" / "Pause subscriber", each opening the standard confirm dialog that prints the exact CLI line (`.llm/runs/dashboard-design--orchestrator/design-prompts/03-capability-consoles.md`, Streams section). The same prompt mandates console empty states be "teaching moments (show the scaffold command), never gated previews" — streams has no scaffold command to show. The prototype's S10 screen currently ships a designed "read-model not wired" empty state that the revamp must replace with the shipped surface (`.llm/runs/dashboard-design--orchestrator/screen-catalog.md` S10; `.llm/runs/dashboard-design--orchestrator/coverage-matrix.md` row #431).

### Dashboard capability unlock

`unblocks-dashboard: true` — every runtime verb the console's confirm dialogs would print is a stub today, and the teaching empty states have no scaffold verb to teach. Once this issue lands: the S10 delivery/fan-out and topic surfaces gain a CLI-parity read seam; the Redeliver/Pause/clear write-actions have real commands behind their confirm dialogs; "New stream" create actions can print `streams add-schema`/`add-producer`; and `/streams/:streamId` health/wiring tabs can render the `inspectStreamTopic` JSON verdict. Until then the entire Streams console (a locked route group in the revamp) is a read-only pane over HTTP with no CLI story.

## Scope

1. Wire `list-topics | subscribe | publish | stats | clear` to `@netscript/plugin-streams-core` (topic walker over `plugins/*/streams/producer.ts`, `inspectStreamTopic`, durable-stream test client).
2. Add `streams add-schema <name> [--collection k=type:primaryKey]` emitting a `defineStreamSchema` module.
3. Add `streams add-producer <name> --stream-path --producer-id` emitting a producer around `createDurableStream`/`createServiceStreamProducer`.
4. Add `streams add-consumer <topic>` emitting a StreamDB factory + query island + server seed loader.
5. Surface `inspectStreamTopic` via `streams inspect <topic>` (JSON output) and the plugin doctor.

## Acceptance

- [x] `netscript streams list-topics` discovers real topics from project producers (non-empty in a scaffolded project with the workers plugin installed) — PR #738: source-based topic walker discovers project + plugin producers (non-empty in tests; scaffold-runtime proof at the orchestrator gate).
- [x] `streams publish` and `subscribe` round-trip a test event against a running streams service — PR #738: real round-trip green against DurableStreamTestServer.
- [x] `streams stats`/`inspect <topic>` emit the `inspectStreamTopic` JSON report; `clear` resets dev state — PR #738: both emit the inspectStreamTopic JSON report; clear resets dev state.
- [x] `streams add-schema` / `add-producer` / `add-consumer` emit compiling modules that the topic walker then discovers — PR #738: generated modules compile (deno check in temp project) and are rediscovered by the walker.
- [x] plugin doctor for streams includes topic health from `inspectStreamTopic` — PR #738: doctor includes topic health from inspectStreamTopic.
- [x] `docs/site/reference/streams/index.md` matches the implemented behavior (no verb documented as shipped while stubbed) — PR #738: reference updated to implemented behavior.
- [x] CLI E2E covers at least list-topics + publish/subscribe against the scaffold runtime suite — PR #738: e2e coverage code included; executed by the orchestrator merge-readiness scaffold-runtime run.

items.title: S1: typed auth contract seam (plugin-auth-core) - zero-cast + JSR S5
items.body:
## S1 — Auth contract seam (`@netscript/plugin-auth-core`)

**Leaf slice** of the auth quality-hardening track. Base: `feat/prime-time/auth` (umbrella #86).
Generated by a WSL Codex daemon-attached slice under the NetScript harness. **Not self-certified** —
this PR awaits a separate-session OpenHands IMPL-EVAL (qwen 3.7 max).

### Root cause fixed
The oRPC contract→handler typing seam was **erased** at the contract package: the contract was built
with `oc`, then cast onto a hand-rolled structural shim, so the real inferred oRPC type never left the
package (forcing `os.router(authV1 as any)` downstream). S1 restores a typed `$context` wrapper so S2
can adopt full handler inference.

### Changes (boundary = `packages/plugin-auth-core` only)
- Replaced the hand-rolled contract/procedure/schema shims with
  `authContractV1 = implement(authContractDefinition) as unknown as AuthContractV1` — the **single**
  centralized assertion permitted by the E2E type-soundness policy.
- Deleted shim interfaces (`AuthContractDefinition`, `AuthContractProcedureLike`, `StandardSchemaLike`,
  `AuthContractSchema`) and the `*SchemaLike` shims in `config`/`domain`/`streams`; signatures now use
  explicit `z.ZodType` public annotations.
- Collapsed the duplicate stream `AuthSession` schema (streams now alias the single `domain` source).
- Added a **compile-time contract test** (`auth.contract_test.ts`) guarding `$context<TContext>()`
  route input/context/error typing and the consolidated session-schema identity, so future drift fails
  `deno check`.
- **JSR §5:** `@module` + `@example` across the full export map; explicit signatures (no slow types);
  `@orpc/server` added to package `deno.json` now that the package owns the `implement()` seam.

### Gate evidence (self-reported in worklog; IMPL-EVAL verifies)
| Gate | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Scoped check / lint / fmt (17 files) | 0 findings |
| Package tests | 19 passed, 0 failed |
| JSR `deno doc --lint` (full export set) | clean |
| JSR `deno publish --dry-run` | no slow-type warnings, clean file list |
| Cast/shim scan | only `as unknown as AuthContractV1` remains |
| `deno.lock` hygiene | clean (`git diff --quiet -- deno.lock`) |

### Cast policy compliance
Zero casts in `plugin-auth-core` except the single centralized `as unknown as AuthContractV1`. No
`as never` / `as any` / hand-rolled shim retained.

Run artifacts: `.llm/tmp/run/feat-prime-time-auth-s1-contract--impl/` (`worklog.md`, `drift.md`,
`context-pack.md`, `commits.md`).

items.title: plan: process-manager plugin seed run (pup/pm2-class, deploy bare-metal target)
items.body:
# Seed run — NetScript process-manager plugin RFC (`plan-process-manager--seed`)

**Planning-only seed run** (`.llm/harness/workflow/seed-run.md` stages A–I). Deliverable = a
ratified RFC epic + sub-issues for a **Deno-native, NetScript-native process manager** (the
pup/pm2 concept done right for 2026), shipped as a **NetScript plugin** (fat Archetype-2 core +
thin plugin), delivered through **two surfaces**: a pure **CLI** (`netscript pm`) and a
**web/desktop admin console** over the same core — and wired in as the **bare-metal deployment
target** of epic #327.

Refs #327 (never a closing keyword on an epic).

## ✅ FILED (Stage H, 2026-07-06) — GitHub is now the single source of truth

Owner ratified all nine forks (OF-1..8 = recommendation (a); **OF-5: clustering → Backlog**;
**OF-9: M1 = beta.8**). One-shot filing complete:

- **Epic #510** (`epic:process-manager`, child of #327, sub-issue linked) + **36 children
  #511–#546** (PM-0..PM-35), all labeled/milestoned and sub-issue-linked under #510.
- **Milestones:** PM-0 → beta.6 (#511) · PM-1..31 + PM-32/33 → beta.8 (#512–#544) · PM-34 →
  stable (#545) · PM-35 clustering → Backlog / Triage (#546).
- **#345 re-scoped** to cross-host HA + secret store + signing (per-host multi-instance moved to
  #546); **2 cross-epic comments on #400** (CR-DDX-HOSTAGNOSTIC + `CommandInvokePort`
  first-definer ack).
- Full PM→issue map: `FILING-LOG.md` in the run dir.

## Locked architecture (research.md §C1, plan.md §1)

Mode-split hybrid: the supervision engine is a **library** in the core; dev (`--no-aspire`) runs it
foreground-attached (`pm dev`, no daemon); production keeps **systemd/Servy as supervisor of
record** via generated native units; the resident prod component is a **control-plane oRPC
service** that is itself one more OS-supervised sibling unit — never a parent of the workload. The
pm2 god-daemon failure class is structurally impossible.

## Stage trail

| Stage | Status | Commit | Proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A Bootstrap | ✅ | `37bcffdc` | charter read-back comment |
| B Discovery (8-topic corpus, Tier C ×8) | ✅ | `102907e5` + `e295ad22` | Stage-B comment |
| C Synthesis (S1–S13, OF-1..7, C7 guards) | ✅ | `c438c650` | Stage-C comment |
| D Design packs D1–D5 (Tier B ×5 + Tier-A full review) | ✅ | `5d3abb0b` | Stage-D comment |
| E Plan lock (`plan.md`) | ✅ | `59b8e93d` | Stage-E comment |
| F Adversarial (fallback lane: OpenHands qwen-3.7-max, unoriented) | ✅ PASS | `0836d821` | Stage-F comment (18 findings triaged: 13 fixed, 2 rejected, 3 notes) |
| G PLAN-EVAL (OpenHands minimax-M3, separate session) | ✅ PASS (first cycle) | `2062a536` (evaluator) + `0de2b741` | verdict comment + Stage-G comment |
| H Owner ratification + one-shot filing | ✅ FILED | see Stage-H comment | epic #510 + #511–#546 |
| I Implementation handoff briefs | in progress | — | — |

## The plan at a glance (`plan.md`, superseded by the filed board)

- **E1–E16 resolved decisions** — headline: no new deploy-target key (the pm implements behind the
  reserved `linux-service`/`windows-service` targets); one state vocabulary; S7 restart floor
  locked; OS-service-layer extraction into **`packages/deploy-core`** (E5 as amended by Stage-F
  F-3; lands at #531 with the F-DEPLOY gate promotion).
- **Slice DAG: PM-0..PM-35 = #511–#546** — 32 milestone-1 slices + 4 deferred, across W0 precursor
  / W1 engine / W2 control plane / W3 deploy + OS adapters / W4 config + scaffold / W5 surfaces;
  the `v1-min` wave marks the cuttable floor (21 slices).
- **Owner forks OF-1..9 ratified 2026-07-06**; **risks R1–R10**; gate matrix (merge-readiness =
  #542: scaffold e2e + JSR-scoped publish dry-run for `@netscript/deploy-core` +
  `@netscript/plugin-process-manager-core`).

## Key artifacts

- `research.md` — corpus index + §C1–C7 synthesis (13 decisions, 7 owner-forks, scope guards)
- `research/design/d1..d5-*.md` — five cited design packs (Σ2873 lines)
- `plan.md` — the Stage-E lock (decisions, DAG, train, risks, gates, filing plan)
- `plan-eval.md` — PLAN-EVAL PASS artifact (evaluator-committed)
- `FILING-LOG.md` — Stage-H filing record (PM→issue map, ratified picks, deltas)
- `worklog.md` / `drift.md` / `context-pack.md`

items.title: [Toolchain] Deno 2.8.x + Aspire 13.4.x upgrade — IMPL (Phase T, type foundation green)
items.body:
## Toolchain upgrade — Phase T IMPL in progress

> **Status:** Research ✅ → Plan ✅ → **Phase T IMPL in progress** — type foundation is green (`deno task check`=0, lint clean, fmt clean); T1/T2/T4/T5 remaining. Greenfield alpha, no backward compat.

Coordinated upgrade of the two framework toolchains. Sequenced into four phases so `main` is green at every merge and a broken `netscript init` is never emitted:

| Phase | Branch (impl) | What |
| ----- | ------------- | ---- |
| **T** | _this branch_ | Deno `2.7.11 → 2.8.x` + folds the `packages/aspire` barrel `deno publish --dry-run` fix (publish prerequisite). CI pin, `catalog:`, slow-types carve-outs, `deno ci`/`audit`. |
| **P** | `chore/jsr-alpha-publish` | Publish **alpha.0** of all 28 members **except `@netscript/cli`** to JSR → unlocks `scaffold.published.runtime` e2e (tests the *production* `netscript init`). |
| **A** | `chore/aspire-13.4-version-bump` | Thin `SCAFFOLD_VERSIONS` bump to 13.4.x. **Decoupled default**, coupled fallback only if 13.4 still preview (gated by E-12 preview guard). |
| (later) | post-13.5 | Native Deno apphost flip (`microsoft/aspire#16218`) — **not a launch gate**, stubbed only. |

### Execution status (Phase T)

**Done on branch** (`fa9ff85`):
- **T0** `f16b31f` — aspire public-barrel value/type export split (LD-1 publish prerequisite).
- **T3** isolatedDeclarations normalization under Deno 2.8 / TS 6.0.3 — `db11fb7` triggers, `212189a` workers, `ac4ee94` plugins (stream+saga), `b64dea1` fresh fixtures, `f44c2da` fresh-ui; the one real `TS2322` (SSE timer handle) fixed via exported `SSEIntervalId`, no `@ts-ignore` (`939bbe9`); `03838d1` fmt. **These are publish-surface annotation gaps 2.8 enforces, not regressions.**
- **LD-10 (new)** `2d5e7ac` — `packages/cli` + `cli/e2e` get a temporary `isolatedDeclarations: false` carve-out (DEBT_ACCEPTED); the CLI publish surface is Wave 6's (A6-v2, #43), so annotating here would collide (LD-8). 5th carve-out, additional to LD-5's four.

**Remaining (resequenced, generator resumes):** **T1** CI pin `v2.8.x` → **T2** `catalog:` + 28-member rewrite → **T4** four `--allow-slow-types` carve-outs + debt rows + 28-member `publish:dry-run` → **T5 (last)** `deno ci` + per-fn coverage + `--parallel`, and the **APPROVED** `deno audit` `@orpc/client` bump (only slice permitted to move `deno.lock`; `vite`/`esbuild` deferred as debt). Phase A (A1–A4) stays gated on Aspire 13.4 GA (LD-7), out of scope here.

Also added repo `.gitattributes` (`*.md`/`*.ts text eol=lf`) to stop Windows-side CRLF churn; the generator now runs as a supervised Codex session in WSL (`/home/codex/repos/netscript-deno28-upgrade`), LF-native.

### Key research finding baked into the plan
The Aspire upgrade is a **CLI scaffold-constant edit, not a `dotnet/` edit** (D-1/D-2): `AppHost.csproj` + `global.json` are *generated* by `netscript init`, so the pins live in `packages/cli/src/kernel/constants/scaffold-versions.ts`. **Single-file ownership (LD-8):** this run owns `scaffold-versions.ts` + the CI pin; Wave 6 (#43) owns `scaffold-files.ts` + the apphost-path realignment. No file is edited by both programs.

### Plan artifacts (updated `fa9ff85`)
- `…/plan.md` — Phase T + Phase A, **10 locked decisions** (LD-10 added), commit slices with status, risk register, E-12 preview guard, **Execution Status (IMPL delta)** section.
- `…/phase-p-jsr-alpha-publish-plan.md` — the in-between JSR alpha.0 publish milestone.
- `…/worklog.md` (§Design + §Implementation log), `…/drift.md` (D-1..D-5 + IMPL-D-1/2/3), `…/commits.md` (landed green-up).

Base: `feat/package-quality`. Runs in parallel with Wave 6 CLI (#43); ownership-disjoint and dependency-linked.

items.title: fix(route): use {} for static-segment path inference (closes #178)
items.body:
## Summary

Closes #178.

`InferRoutePatternSegment` returned `EmptyRecord` (= `Record<string, never>`) for static route segments. When intersected via `&` in `InferRoutePatternPathSegments`, the index signature `[k: string]: never` collapsed all dynamic-segment fields to `never`, breaking the public API:

```ts
const channel = createRouteReference('/channel/[id]');
channel.href({ path: { id: 'c-123' } });
// before: TS2322 — `{ id: string }` is not assignable to `never`
// after:  works
```

This is a regression introduced during the netscript-start → netscript port. The prior implementation in `netscript-start/packages/fresh/route/contract.ts:65-74` already used `{}` for static segments and wrapped the intersection in `Simplify<>`. This PR restores that.

## Diff

### `packages/fresh/src/application/route/types.ts`

```diff
 /** Infer one typed path segment from a Fresh route pattern segment. */
 export type InferRoutePatternSegment<TSegment extends string> = TSegment extends
   `[[...${infer TParam}]]` ? { [TKey in TParam]?: readonly string[] }
   : TSegment extends `[...${infer TParam}]` ? { [TKey in TParam]: readonly string[] }
   : TSegment extends `[${infer TParam}]` ? { [TKey in TParam]: string }
-  : EmptyRecord;
+  : {};

 /** Infer typed path params from the stripped Fresh route pattern. */
 export type InferRoutePatternPathSegments<TPattern extends string> = TPattern extends ''
   ? EmptyRecord
   : TPattern extends `${infer TSegment}/${infer TRest}`
-    ? InferRoutePatternSegment<TSegment> & InferRoutePatternPathSegments<TRest>
+    ? InferRoutePatternSegment<TSegment> & InferRoutePatternPathSegments<TRest> & {}
   : InferRoutePatternSegment<TPattern>;
```

### `packages/fresh/src/application/route/_internal/contract-types.ts`

```diff
 type InferRoutePatternSegment<TSegment extends string> = TSegment extends `[[...${infer TParam}]]`
   ? { [TKey in TParam]?: readonly string[] }
   : TSegment extends `[...${infer TParam}]` ? { [TKey in TParam]: readonly string[] }
   : TSegment extends `[${infer TParam}]` ? { [TKey in TParam]: string }
-  : EmptyRecord;
+  : {};
```

(Line 78 in the internal file already wraps the intersection in `Simplify<>`, so no further change is needed there. In `types.ts` we deliberately use `& {}` rather than introducing a new `Simplify` import — the trailing empty object literal flattens the intersection identically without expanding the file's dependency surface. `EmptyRecord` itself is preserved — it remains the discriminator for "no path params" used at `types.ts:162-171` and many other places.)

## Validation cases

All six cases from the issue's validation table are now covered by direct (non-cast) compile-time assertions in `packages/fresh/src/application/route/contract.test.ts`:

| Pattern | Expected `InferRoutePatternPath` |
|---|---|
| `/channel/[id]` | `{ id: string }` |
| `/session/[a]/[b]` | `{ a: string; b: string }` |
| `/[id]` (single dynamic, no static prefix) | `{ id: string }` |
| `/static/only` | `{}` |
| `/posts/[[...slug]]` | `{ slug?: readonly string[] }` |
| `/dashboard/products/[id]/edit` | `{ id: string }` |

The existing runtime cases in `contract.test.ts:176-235` continue to pass.

## How to verify

1. From the repo root, run the scoped package check (the wrapper-scoped gate that CI runs):

   ```bash
   deno task check
   ```

2. Type-check the affected surface directly:

   ```bash
   deno check --quiet --unstable-kv packages/fresh/src
   ```

3. Run the route contract tests:

   ```bash
   deno test --allow-all --no-check packages/fresh/src/application/route/contract.test.ts
   ```

   Expected: `10 passed | 0 failed`, including the new `InferRoutePatternPath infers static segments as {} (regression for #178)` test.

4. Verify the session-workspace repro from the issue no longer errors. The published JSR repro at `C:\Users\chaut\.copilot\session-state\2004fad4-33a3-4744-9a50-25e4267cc7b4\files\repro\bug-typecheck.ts` imports from `jsr:@netscript/fresh@0.0.1-alpha.12/route`, so it requires a published fix. A workspace-local equivalent (importing from the patched source) compiles cleanly against this branch:

   ```bash
   deno check --config deno.json <(echo "import { createRouteReference } from 'packages/fresh/src/application/route/mod.ts'; const c = createRouteReference('/channel/[id]'); type I = NonNullable<Parameters<typeof c.href>[0]>; type P = I extends { path: infer P } ? P : never; const ok: P = { id: 'c-123' };")
   ```

   Expected: no `TS2322` error.

## Regression coverage

I verified that the new direct assertions catch the bug by reverting the patch and re-running `deno check packages/fresh/src/application/route/contract.test.ts` — without the patch, four of the six patterns fail with `TS2322` (`/channel/[id]`, `/session/[a]/[b]`, `/posts/[[...slug]]`, `/dashboard/products/[id]/edit`). With the patch applied, all ten tests pass and `deno check` is clean.

## Out of scope

- **WI-12** codegen work — separate effort that depends on this landing first. Not touched.
- **JSR package version** — not bumped. Will be cut in the next release once this lands.
- The PR is opened as **DRAFT** — only the plan-eval session and the user decide on merging.

## References

- #178 — this fix (implementation tracking)
- #177 — original user report (analysis chain)
- WI-12 — downstream codegen work that depends on this landing

milestone.title: 0.0.1-beta.1
milestone.description:
First beta of the 0.0.1 line — a MINIMAL cuttable release of the current green state. The 0.0.1 line ships on an incremental-beta cadence (beta.1 -> beta.2 -> ... -> stable), mirroring the alpha line (alpha.1..alpha.19: 19 incremental prereleases). beta.1 is NOT a positioning gate; it publishes the framework as it stands today so the beta channel opens and later bets land incrementally.

Cut bar (all satisfied on main as of 2026-07-03):
1. Soundness floor — plugin-service contract seam type-sound end-to-end (172a-2-SOUND, #332 CLOSED).
2. Instrument honesty — bench `conformance` gate green (cast-free NetScript reference passes the frozen HTTP suite 10/10 with a real process restart proving @netscript/kv persistence; CI-guarded every run). Satisfied floor, not blocked work.
3. Scaffold trust — `scaffold.runtime` e2e green (48/0).
4. CI trio green — quality / check-test / deps-report succeed on main.
5. Release machinery works — `deno task release:cut` (workspace+lock bump, preflight, publish:dry-run, ci --prod) and the `publish.yml` OIDC publish path, both proven across alpha.1..alpha.19.

MOVED OUT of beta.1 (were in the prior LOCKED heavy bar):
- self-bench t1+t2 test_pass_rate >=0.90 median -> beta.2 (regression-detector quality bar; blocked on bench decisions D5/OQ2).
- competitor-PAIRED run, multi-task breadth, full composite rubric -> 0.0.1-stable (the falsifiable positioning verdict).

Note: allow-slow-types is a sanctioned doctrine exception for oRPC-bound packages (S2; S4/#305) and does not count against the soundness/publish bar. Positioning verdict is a stable-line goal reached incrementally; see packages/bench/POSITIONING.md (owned by the road-to-stable lane).

items.body:
## 172a-2-SOUND · Slice 3 of 4 — first real soundness enforcement

Refs #332

Migrates the CRUD generator and the workers/sagas health routers off the transitional erasure and onto the sound `BaseContractRoute` / `BaseContractOutputRoute` aliases landed in Slice 1 (#334). This is the **first slice where the seam actually rejects unsound handlers** — proven by a CI-enforced negative fixture.

Slice 4 (drop the `@deprecated` transitional aliases + scaffold template, gated by `scaffold.runtime` e2e) is the one that carries `Closes #332`. The `@deprecated` aliases in `contract-primitives.ts` are intentionally left intact here.

### What changed (4 files)

- `packages/contracts/crud/create-crud-contract.ts` — removed the `LegacyBaseContractBuilder` structural shim and the `soundBaseContract as unknown as LegacyBaseContractBuilder` cast that Slice 1 had relocated here. The generator now drives the base contract through the sound types. **Repo-wide `as unknown as` count drops by exactly 1** (the relocated cast is now deleted, not moved). The package's 5 sanctioned ContractSchema↔Zod bridging casts are untouched.
- `plugins/workers/services/src/routers/health.ts`, `plugins/sagas/services/src/routers/health.ts` — each route annotated `BaseContractRoute<typeof InSchema, typeof OutSchema>` with explicitly-typed named schema consts (required by root `isolatedDeclarations`). Handler bodies are now genuinely output-checked.
- `plugins/workers/services/src/routers/health-soundness_test.ts` (new) — type-level negative fixture, matching the repo's existing `@ts-expect-error`-guarded `_test.ts` convention and reachable by the workspace `deno check` graph.

### Verification (reproduced independently in a separate evaluator session)

| Gate | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `deno task check` (whole workspace, 1991 files) | **0 failures** — all consumers compile |
| scoped lint (`packages/contracts` + `plugins/workers` + `plugins/sagas`) | **0** |
| scoped fmt (same roots) | **0** |
| `deno task publish:dry-run` | **Success**; `packages/contracts/deno.json` **byte-identical** to origin/main — `--allow-slow-types` unchanged, no new slow-types flags |
| repo-wide `as unknown as` delta | **−1** (`LegacyBaseContractBuilder` fully removed; 0 occurrences on HEAD) |

**Negative-fixture proof (the load-bearing check).** With the `@ts-expect-error` guards in place the fixture passes `deno check`. Strip the guards and `deno check` **fails with exactly 2 `TS2322` errors** on the two wrong handlers:
- `Type '"on-fire"' is not assignable to type '"healthy" | "unhealthy"'` (wrong enum literal)
- `Type '{}' is not assignable to type 'Promisable<{ status: "healthy" | "unhealthy"; }>'` (missing required field)

The second diagnostic's origin is `@orpc/server`'s `ProcedureHandler` return type, confirming the check flows through `implement()` — the exact handler-body soundness the 172a-2-SOUND blocker is about. Under the pre-Slice-1 `~orpc: any` erasure both handlers compiled silently.

**Doc-lint (disclosed, non-blocking, unchanged by this slice).** `deno doc --lint packages/contracts/mod.ts` reports 8 `private-type-ref` diagnostics on `BaseContractRoute`/`BaseContractOutputRoute` — these originate in Slice-1 code that this diff does **not** touch, they pre-exist on origin/main, and they are waived at publish by the pre-existing `--allow-slow-types` (publish:dry-run passes). They are the intrinsic cost of binding to oRPC's real `declare`d internal types.

### Not in scope (tracked)
- Slice 4: drop the transitional `@deprecated` aliases + scaffold template update (gated by `scaffold.runtime` e2e; `Closes #332`).
- Slice 5 (optional): rebind the bench reference; re-evaluate contracts `--allow-slow-types`.

items.title: fix(cli): JSR-safe bundled-asset embedding — prod CLI scaffold usable from JSR (alpha.5)
items.body:
## Why

The published `@netscript/cli` was **unusable from JSR**: scaffold loaders read bundled assets via
`Deno.readTextFile`/`fromFileUrl` on `import.meta.url`-relative paths, which throws `Must be a file
URL` when the module is served over `https://` from JSR. This is the systemic prod blocker behind
the alpha.5 cut — assets *ship*, but the read mechanism is the bug.

Fix class (locked mechanism): bundled assets travel as **importable content** via generated
barrels, not filesystem reads — the durable JSR-safe embedding pattern.

## What

- **S1 — template registry**: generate `packages/cli` template-registry barrel; CLI scaffolder
  consumes embedded content; removed the `import.meta`-relative `Deno.readTextFile` default.
- **S2 — plugin scaffold templates**: same embedding for plugin scaffold templates; `editor-config`
  / template-asset / contract templates no longer crash on import over https.
- **S3 — fresh-ui registry**: `deno task gen:assets-barrel` now also generates
  `packages/fresh-ui/registry.generated.ts`; new `@netscript/fresh-ui/registry` public subpath
  exports the manifest + content; CLI UI installer uses embedded fresh-ui content by default
  (`--registry-root` remains the explicit filesystem override).
- **plugin doc-lint dep types** exposed so the full export map lints clean.

## Validation (from the harness worklog)

| Gate | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `deno task check:assets-barrel` | PASS — cli + plugin + fresh-ui barrels diff clean |
| focused tests (template-asset / scaffolder / ui registry) | PASS — 18 tests |
| scoped check `packages/cli packages/plugin packages/fresh-ui` | PASS — 712 files, 0 diagnostics |
| scoped fmt / lint (S3 files) | PASS |
| plugin full-export-map `deno doc --lint` (8 modules) | PASS |
| cli / plugin / fresh-ui `publish:dry-run` | PASS (existing dynamic-import warnings only) |
| **`e2e:cli run scaffold.runtime --cleanup`** | **PASS — 47 passed, 0 failed** |

Known non-blocker: `deno doc --lint` over the **full fresh-ui export map** surfaces **87
pre-existing** runtime public-surface doc/private-type findings (`mod.ts`/`interactive.ts`/
`primitives.tsx`) — **no `registry` findings**. Tracked separately; out of scope for the asset-read
fix.

Base: `main` @ alpha.4 (`98b087ef`); 5 commits ahead, 0 behind — clean.

items.title: Add OpenHands agent handoff workflow
items.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a committed OpenHands foundation for NetScript so local agents, GitHub Actions, and a long-running VPS/Web UI deployment can use the same handoff protocol.

The branch is based directly on `origin/main`, not on `feat/package-quality`, so it is intended to merge cleanly into `main` after review.

## Why

The recent PR #18 workflow showed the real operating pattern we want to preserve:

- trigger a cloud agent from the PR thread with a model-qualified instruction,
- include `use harness` when the task is an evaluator or harness phase,
- handle legitimate Augment/Copilot review comments before writing the final verdict,
- publish a PR comment that summarizes fixes, validations, and remaining risks.

This PR turns that pattern into repository-owned automation and documentation instead of relying on local IDE/agent behavior.

## Implementation

### 1. GitHub Actions OpenHands runner

Adds `.github/workflows/openhands-agent.yml`.

Supported triggers:

- manual `workflow_dispatch` with prompt, model, target PR, and output mode,
- issue or PR labels: `fix-me`, `openhands`, or `agent:<profile-or-model>`,
- trusted PR/issue comments containing `@openhands-agent`,
- commit messages containing `[openhands ...]`.

The comment trigger is gated to `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, or `COLLABORATOR` author associations because the job has write permissions.

Per-run model selection precedence:

1. manual workflow `model` input,
2. `model=...` in comment or commit message,
3. `agent=<profile>` in comment or commit message,
4. `agent:<profile-or-literal>` label,
5. repository variable `OPENHANDS_DEFAULT_MODEL`,
6. fallback `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`.

Profiles currently map to:

- `sonnet` -> `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`,
- `gpt` -> `openai/gpt-5.1`,
- `gemini` -> `gemini/gemini-2.5-pro`.

Output modes:

- `pr-comment`: one summary comment,
- `respond-comments`: one summary comment that explicitly addresses review/issue comments,
- `thread-replies`: summary plus optional PR review-thread replies from `.llm/tmp/openhands/replies.json`,
- `summary-only`: upload artifacts without commenting.

### 2. OpenHands SDK runner and repo bootstrap

Adds `.openhands/agent_runner.py`, `.openhands/setup.sh`, and `.openhands/microagents/repo.md`.

The workflow uses the current OpenHands SDK pattern for custom task execution instead of only a fixed resolver action. That gives us explicit control over prompt composition, required summary artifacts, fetched PR comment context, and optional threaded replies.

The setup script mirrors the existing Copilot setup assumptions:

- Deno 2,
- .NET 10,
- Aspire CLI,
- Docker verification,
- Deno dependency cache warming,
- NuGet/workload visibility.

### 3. Handoff protocol documentation

Adds `AGENTS-handoff.md`.

This is the human/agent-facing protocol for:

- triggering cloud runs from labels, comments, commit messages, and Actions dispatch,
- choosing a model per run,
- choosing output behavior,
- preserving the PAT-vs-`GITHUB_TOKEN` chain-trigger rule,
- handling Augment/Copilot review comments,
- deciding when to use Actions versus the VPS/Web UI session.

### 4. Local-agent skill and harness integration

Adds `.agents/skills/openhands-handoff/SKILL.md` and `.llm/harness/workflow/agent-handoff.md`.

Updates:

- `.agents/skills/netscript-harness/SKILL.md` now points harness-aware agents to the OpenHands handoff workflow when a run crosses local/cloud/VPS boundaries.
- `.llm/harness/README.md` now states that GitHub comments are supporting evidence, not replacements for harness artifacts.

The important harness invariant is preserved: PLAN-EVAL and IMPL-EVAL still require separate sessions. An OpenHands cloud run may perform one role, but it must not self-certify work it generated in the same session.

### 5. VPS / Dokploy scaffold

Adds `ops/openhands/docker-compose.yml` and `ops/openhands/.env.example`.

This gives the long-running OpenHands Web UI path a committed starting point for Dokploy or direct Docker Compose deployment. The intended split is:

- Actions workflow for short PR/issue tasks, evaluator passes, review-comment fixes, and mobile triggers,
- VPS Web UI/SDK session for longer multi-step work that needs pause/resume or human-in-the-loop steering.

## Required GitHub configuration

Repository secrets:

- `LLM_API_KEY_<PROVIDER>` preferred, selected automatically from the resolved provider.
- `LLM_API_KEY` optional fallback when a provider-specific key is absent.
- `LLM_BASE_URL_<PROVIDER>` optional for provider-specific OpenAI-compatible gateways.
- `LLM_BASE_URL` optional fallback base URL.
- `PAT_TOKEN` strongly recommended when cloud-created comments, commits, or labels should trigger follow-up workflows.

Common provider-specific secrets:

| Selected model/provider | Secret used first | Optional base URL key |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `anthropic/...` | `LLM_API_KEY_ANTHROPIC` | `LLM_BASE_URL_ANTHROPIC` |
| `openai/...` | `LLM_API_KEY_OPENAI` | `LLM_BASE_URL_OPENAI` |
| `gemini/...` or `google/...` | `LLM_API_KEY_GEMINI` | `LLM_BASE_URL_GEMINI` |
| `openrouter/...` or `provider=openrouter` | `LLM_API_KEY_OPENROUTER` | `LLM_BASE_URL_OPENROUTER` |

Repository variable:

- `OPENHANDS_DEFAULT_MODEL` optional, defaults to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`.

The workflow infers provider from the selected model prefix unless the trigger includes `provider=...`.
Example: `@openhands-agent provider=openrouter model=openai/gpt-5.1 ...` uses `LLM_API_KEY_OPENROUTER` while keeping the selected model explicit.

## Validation

Ran on this branch:

- `deno fmt --check .github/workflows/openhands-agent.yml AGENTS-handoff.md .agents/skills/openhands-handoff/SKILL.md .llm/harness/workflow/agent-handoff.md .openhands/microagents/repo.md ops/openhands/docker-compose.yml ops/openhands/.env.example .agents/skills/netscript-harness/SKILL.md .llm/harness/README.md` PASS.
- `python -B -m py_compile .openhands/agent_runner.py` PASS.
- `deno eval --no-lock "import { parse } from 'jsr:@std/yaml'; for (const path of ['.github/workflows/openhands-agent.yml','ops/openhands/docker-compose.yml']) parse(await Deno.readTextFile(path));"` PASS.

Deno emitted pre-existing workspace warnings from `examples/playground/deno.json` about `nodeModulesDir`, `unstable`, and `workspace` fields being root-only. They are unrelated to this PR.

## Notes for reviewers

This PR intentionally does not depend on the `feat/package-quality` branch. During PR prep, the work was moved onto a clean branch from `origin/main` to keep the merge path direct.

The workflow uses a trusted-comment gate because the job has write permissions. Labels and manual dispatch are already permission-gated by GitHub.

items.title: feat(fresh): restore inline .withRouteContract shorthand + codegen page-module route binding (WI-12)
items.body:
## Summary

Implements **WI-12 planning v2** — restores the inline `.withRouteContract({ pathSchema?, searchSchema?, paths? })` shorthand on `definePage()` and extends the Vite plugin's codegen pass so that **neither** the sidecar form **nor** the inline form require the author to write the binding call. The generator inserts it.

The v2 plan makes the intentional two-tree model explicit:

- **`routePatterns.<key>` in `.generated/manifest.ts`** is the raw route-pattern string consumed by `bindRoutePattern(contract, routePattern, ...)`.
- **`routes.<key>` in `.generated/routes.ts`** is the bound reference consumed by `.withRoute(boundRef)`.

Three forms converge on the same `.generated/routes.ts` output:

- **Form A (inline)** — `.withRouteContract({ pathSchema: ..., searchSchema: ..., paths: ... })` is hand-written by the user; generator inserts or validates optional `$route: routePatterns.<key>.$route`.
- **Form B (sidecar)** — sibling `routes/.../<page>.route.ts` is hand-written; generator inserts `.withRoute(routes.<key>.$route)`.
- **Form C (no contract)** — generator inserts a default `.withRoute(routes.<key>.$route)` backed by `createRouteReference(routePattern)`.

A new Vite plugin option `pageModuleRouteBinding: true | false` (default `true`) disables page-module rewriting for migration safety.

## Cross-link

- **#181** — this implementation tracking issue (WI-12)
- **#178 / #179** — `InferRoutePatternSegment` EmptyRecord regression. WI-12 depends on #179 because the codegen-emitted `.withRoute(routes.<key>.$route)` chain relies on `createRouteReference`'s path-param inference being correct. Without #179, scaffolded pages with dynamic segments would surface the same `TS2322` from typed `Link`/`href`.

## Plan

### Phase A — Foundation (no behavior change yet)

1. **Restore `.withRouteContract({...})` builder method** on `definePage()`.
   - Add to `packages/fresh/src/application/builders/define-page/builder/mod.tsx` (or `route-support.ts`).
   - Accepts `{ pathSchema?, searchSchema?, paths? }`; `$route` is optional in user source and generator-owned/validated.
   - Type-state promotion: the method promotes the builder to the same "routed" state as `.withRoute(...)` so subsequent calls compile identically.

2. **Manifest types** — extend `packages/fresh/src/application/route/manifest-types.ts` with a pending route-binding descriptor.

### Phase B — Generator + Vite plugin

3. **Generator: synthesize inline contract from page module AST** in `packages/fresh/src/application/route/manifest.ts`.
   - Extract inline `pathSchema`, `searchSchema`, and `paths` fields from `.withRouteContract({...})`.
   - Emit stable `routeContract<N> = defineRouteContract({ ...inlineSchemaFields })` consts in `.generated/routes.ts` using discovery order sorted by file path.
   - Bind Form A with `bindRoutePattern(routeContract<N>, routePatterns.<key>, {...})`.

4. **Vite plugin: page-module rewriting (Form A/B/C)** in `packages/fresh/src/application/vite/vite.ts`.
   - Add `pageModuleRouteBinding: true | false` option, default `true`.
   - Dev mode: `transform()` runs on save, skips idempotent content, and does not touch `.generated/manifest.ts` or `.generated/routes.ts`.
   - Build mode: manifest writer runs first during plugin init; page-module rewrite runs during the Rollup `transform()` pass.
   - HMR safety: AST diff is computed against disk and skips already-matching hand-written content.

5. **Generator: Form C (no-contract default)** — for page modules with no inline `.withRouteContract` and no sibling sidecar, emit `createRouteReference(routePatterns.<key>)` in `.generated/routes.ts` and insert `.withRoute(routes.<key>.$route)` in the page module.

6. **Generator: conflict resolution** —
   - Inline `.withRouteContract` + sibling sidecar → Form A wins. Sidecar stays. Build warning: "Inline form takes precedence. Delete the sidecar to silence this warning." Sidecar is NOT auto-deleted.
   - `.withRoute(...)` + `.withRouteContract({...})` in same file → build error: "Pick one."

### Phase C — Tests + migration proof

7. **Unit tests**:
   - Builder typing/runtime cases append to `packages/fresh/src/application/builders/define-page/tests/builder.test.tsx`.
   - Manifest AST discovery cases append to `packages/fresh/src/application/route/manifest.test.ts`.
   - Vite page-module rewriting, idempotency, and option-gate cases append to `packages/fresh/src/application/vite/vite.test.ts`.

8. **Migration proof** — migrate these 6 CLI scaffold template files:
   - Form A: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/dashboard.tsx.template`
   - Form A: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/examples/index.tsx.template`
   - Form A: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/examples/crud.tsx.template`
   - Form B: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/index.tsx.template`
   - Form B: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/examples/telemetry/index.tsx.template`
   - Form B: `packages/cli/src/kernel/assets/app/routes/(design)/design/components.tsx.template`

### Phase D — Docs

9. **Update `packages/fresh/README.md`** — show Form A/B/C with codegen as the primary example. The current README shows manual `bindRoutePattern(...)` as primary, which is misleading.
10. **Update `docs/site/web-layer/builders.md`** — document `.withRouteContract({...})` alongside `.withRoute(boundRoute)`.

## Validation gates

- `deno task check:packages` — must stay green
- New unit tests pass in the canonical files listed above
- CLI scaffold runtime E2E proves the template migration by generating real app routes under `.llm/tmp/cli-e2e/...`
- Scaffolded app typecheck/build gates stay green

## Out of scope (non-goals)

- Re-introducing page-module-export contract discovery (`export const myContract = defineRouteContract(...)` from page-module exports). This was the duplicate discovery path WI-09F removed and stays removed.
- Changing the sidecar discovery algorithm. Stays exactly as today.
- Removing deprecated builder APIs (`defineListPage`, `defineDetailPage`, `defineFormPage`).
- Changing the `withRoute(boundRoute)` API signature. Only its emitter (the page-module writer) changes.
- Auto-deleting sidecar files when inline takes precedence. Sidecar files are left in place; build emits a warning; author decides.

## Status

DRAFT PR. Docs-only v2 planning revision; implementation has not started and should be dispatched separately after plan-eval accepts this v2.

items.title: Migrate shared Zod datetime codecs to Temporal
items.body:
`packages/shared/utils/zod/codecs.ts` still exposed Date-backed datetime codecs. The shared compatibility codecs now align with the repo’s Temporal migration.

- **Temporal codecs**
  - Replaced Date-returning datetime codecs with `Temporal.Instant` codecs:
    - `isoDatetimeToInstant`
    - `epochSecondsToInstant`
    - `epochMillisToInstant`
  - Removed `Date`, `z.date()`, `new Date()`, `getTime()`, and `toISOString()` usage from the codec module.

- **Codec typing**
  - Added explicit codec type annotations so the compatibility module can be checked directly under isolated declarations.

- **Coverage**
  - Added focused round-trip tests for ISO datetime strings, epoch seconds, and epoch milliseconds.

```ts
const instant = isoDatetimeToInstant.decode('2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z');

instant instanceof Temporal.Instant; // true
isoDatetimeToInstant.encode(instant); // "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
```

scheme, **no per-package version bump** — the repo-wide breaking bump lands once at JSR-publish prep
